reels.caption: 🇧🇷 O Daukhaus parece mais limpo na trocação, mas o problema é sobreviver às entradas constantes do Bo Nickal. Se encosta
good: Bold red text hook immediately establishes fight preview topic and stops scrolls
Expert commentary format drives exceptional shareability (126 shares = 8.4% of views, well above typical 1-3%)
Excellent retention at 28.8s suggests analysis narrative holds viewer attention through most of video
Clear matchup framing (Nickal pressure vs Daukhaus striking) gives viewers a specific prediction to debate and share
bad: Language mixing (Portuguese/English) fragments audience and feels incomplete (caption cuts off at 'the real prob')
Multiple analyst frames (frames 2-3) before returning to fight footage creates pacing drag
Weak ending: shows exhaustion without resolving the prediction, leaving viewers unsatisfied rather than compelled to comment
reels.top_tip: End with the explicit prediction/winner call and a direct engagement CTA ('Who's your pick?' or 'Drop your prediction below'). The expert analysis + matchup format is driving your shares — give viewers a reason to add their take in comments.

reels.caption: 🇧🇷 Bonfim tem talento pra virar um dos maiores problemas da divisão. Mas o Belal transforma qualquer luta em pressão e d
good: Strong visual hook: shirtless athlete with confident expression and high-contrast night lighting immediately stops scrolls
Clear narrative arc: Bonfim talent setup → Belal pressure/grind strategy → technical question creates intrigue across frames
Direct engagement question: 'Bonfim is more dangerous in scrambles, right?' invites immediate comment and discussion
Consistent branding: Peace Grappler logo visible throughout, strong branded outro with social handles
bad: Critically low save rate (0.18%)—138 shares but only 4 saves shows content reads as entertainment, not reference/tactics material
Hook text delayed: strongest narrative hook ('biggest problem') doesn't appear until frame 2, risking early scroll-offs before frame 1 locks attention
Missing on-screen tactical labels: scramble position in frame 3 shown but not explained visually—lost opportunity to add breakdownable value
reels.top_tip: Add on-screen tactical labels at key moments (e.g., 'Scramble Advantage: Bonfim Strength' / 'Belal's Pressure Game') to reframe as tactical breakdown vs opinion piece—converts entertainment viewers into save/reference behavior

reels.caption: 🇧🇷 Diego é muito perigoso quando consegue encurtar e misturar pressão com grappling. Mas no centro, a velocidade do Garc
good: Bold red hook text + combat action immediately signals prediction debate content to scrollers
Prediction format generates strong shares (5.6% share rate from 105 shares on 1858 views) — high debate value for MMA audience
Bilingual captions + branded talent (Peace Grappler logos) expand reach and reinforce brand identity
8.1s average watch time shows narrative structure (action → expert context → action) holds viewers through talking-head explanation
bad: Only 2 saves despite 34% engagement — commentary needs visual references to become bookmarkable content
Talking-head frames (2-3) plain without on-screen graphics or comparison visuals to illustrate analysis
No CTA or interactive element visible — relies on organic shares only, missing prompts to drive follows or saves
reels.top_tip: Layer on-screen comparison graphics during commentary ('Speed at range' vs 'Pressure in clinch') to transform shareable prediction into reference material worth saving and rewatching

reels.caption: 🇺🇸Very few fighters reach the top. Even fewer do it by knocking out names like Volkanovski, Holloway and Oliveira. These
good: Exceptional hook: UFC action immediately fills frame with high visual impact and clear branding pulls viewers in
Strong engagement rate (69.5%) with 8.8s average watch time shows content holds attention effectively
Top-5 list format creates curiosity and drives viewers through the reel to see all knockouts
Direct call-to-action in caption ('Which was your favorite? Leave your opinion') encourages comment engagement
bad: Low save rate (1 from 684 views) suggests content lacks 'bookmark-worthy' or collectable quality
Modest share rate (1.3%) despite high engagement indicates limited viral/pass-along potential
Hook text overlay could be 30-40% larger to maximize scroll-stopping dominance in the first frame
reels.top_tip: Amplify the hook with larger, bolder text and feature the single most visually devastating knockout (slow-motion effect or dramatic angle) in the first 1.5 seconds to increase save and share rates

reels.caption: 🇧🇷 Gabriel Bonfim, Joanderson Brito, Bruno Silva, Ketlen Souza, Ariane Carnelossi, Priscila Cachoeira e mais brasileiros
good: Strong visual hook: fighter in action with close-up face immediately grabs attention
Excellent engagement rate (51%) shows content resonates with viewers who see it
Good watch time (5.8s): audience stays to see the fighters and matchups
Clear text hierarchy: white primary text + red accent creates legible, branded look
bad: Extremely low shares (5) and saves (1) despite decent engagement—content isn't sticky or discussion-driving
Modest reach (524) for 822 views suggests hook isn't scroll-stopping enough in feed
Informational tone lacks emotional stakes: announcement of fighters, not debate over who wins
No front-loaded CTA in visuals (only in caption): missed opportunity to push engagement on screen
reels.top_tip: Add on-screen debate hook in first 3 frames (e.g., 'WHO WINS?' or 'UPSET ALERT 🔥') + tag-a-friend CTA to convert high engagement rate into shares—people are watching but not compelled to save/share without emotional investment

reels.caption: 🇺🇸 Gabriel Bonfim moves from #11 to the UFC Welterweight Top 5 after a dominant decision win over Belal Muhammad. The ca
good: Strong hook: UFC branding and fighter names instantly visible with bold text overlay
Excellent engagement rate (54.6%)—content is highly compelling for viewers who interact
Professional production: clean lighting, proper cage framing, branded aesthetic
Solid average watch time (4.0s) indicates viewers staying through content
bad: Very low shares (1 from 453 views)—despite high engagement, content isn't being shared
Minimal saves (1)—viewers engage but don't find it reference-worthy or save-worthy
Reach only 72% of views—algorithm isn't heavily amplifying despite high interaction
Early text overlays ('TKO R1 1:25', fighter name) are too small for mobile viewers
reels.top_tip: Add explicit call-to-action to caption to convert engagement into shares: 'Tag someone you'd pick for your fight card' or 'Who had the most brutal finish?' to unlock reach, since high engagement isn't translating to shares or saves
